post.summary: Privacy-conscious person lookup with journey tracking
post.description:
Search for a person by email, phone, internal ID, or external ID and retrieve their complete journey
through the sales pipeline (lead → contact → opportunity).

## Search Options

Provide at least one of:
- **email**: Search by email address
- **phone**: Search by phone number
- **id**: Search by internal CRM record ID (lead or contact)
- **external_id**: Search by external/partner reference ID

## Privacy Notice

This endpoint returns **only aggregate activity metrics**. It does NOT expose:

- Names, IDs, or any identifying information
- Actual comment content or details
- Owner/assigned user information
- Deal values or commission-sensitive data
- Tags or custom fields

## Rate Limits

- **60 requests per minute** per auth token
- Rate limited to prevent abuse

## Search Logic

1. If `id` or `external_id` provided, searches directly by ID (takes priority)
2. Falls back to email/phone search if no ID match or no ID provided
3. Searches contacts first (prioritizes existing relationships)
4. Searches leads (for new prospects)
5. If a contact is found, searches for associated opportunities
6. Returns the complete journey with activity metrics at each stage

## Use Cases

- **Duplicate Detection**: Check if a person already exists before creating a new lead
- **Journey Tracking**: Understand where a person is in the sales pipeline
- **Commission Attribution**: Verify activity within inactivity period for commission eligibility
- **Direct Lookup**: Retrieve journey info for a known record by ID

## Inactivity Period

The `inactivity_days` parameter (default: 30) determines whether activity is considered
"recent". The `has_recent_activity` response field indicates if the most recent
activity falls within this threshold.

parameters.name: inactivity_days

schema.description: Number of days to consider for inactivity threshold. Activity within this period sets has_recent_activity=true.
schema.title: Inactivity Days

parameters.description: Number of days to consider for inactivity threshold. Activity within this period sets has_recent_activity=true.

422.description: Validation error (invalid email/phone format or missing both)

detail.message: Rate limit exceeded (60 requests per minute)
detail.hint: Please wait before making more requests

post.summary: Paginated leads lookup by marketing source
post.description:
Query leads by marketing source (lead_source) with pagination and journey tracking.

## Search Options

- **lead_source**: Required. The marketing source to filter by (exact match)
- **include_converted**: Include leads converted to contacts (default: true)
- **include_disqualified**: Include disqualified leads (default: false)

## Pagination

- **page_size**: Number of items per page (default: 50, max: 100)
- **page_num**: Page number, 1-indexed (default: 1)

## Privacy Notice

This endpoint returns **only aggregate activity metrics** per lead. It does NOT expose:

- Names or personal identifying information
- Actual comment content or details
- Owner/assigned user information
- Deal values or commission-sensitive data

## Use Cases

- **Partner Analytics**: View all leads from your marketing source
- **Journey Tracking**: Track conversion progress of your leads
- **Performance Monitoring**: Monitor lead-to-opportunity conversion rates

## Inactivity Period

The `inactivity_days` parameter (default: 30) determines whether activity is considered
"recent". The `has_recent_activity` field indicates if the most recent
activity falls within this threshold.

parameters.name: inactivity_days

schema.description: Number of days to consider for inactivity threshold
schema.title: Inactivity Days

parameters.description: Number of days to consider for inactivity threshold

detail.hint: Please wait before making more requests

post.description:
Partner login endpoint.

Partners authenticate using their partner_id and access_code
to receive their API token for subsequent requests.

**Request:**
```
POST /crm/partner/login?partner_id=TRIKIN3847&access_code=847291
```

**Response:**
```json
{
    "token": "api_token_for_requests",
    "partner_name": "Trikin",
    "allowed_marketing_sources": ["Trikin", "Trikin-FB"],
    "usage": "Use 'Authorization: Partner <token>' for API requests"
}
```

get.description:
Get leads filtered by partner's allowed marketing sources.

Partners can ONLY retrieve leads that match their configured
`allowed_marketing_sources`. This filter is automatically applied.

**Authorization:** `Partner <token>`

**Response includes:**
- `is_converted`: Whether lead has been converted to contact
- `contact_id`: Contact ID if converted
- `has_deal`: Whether a deal/opportunity exists
- `deal_stage`: Current deal stage (if exists)
- `deal_id`: Deal/Opportunity ID (if exists)

**Example:**
```
GET /crm/partner/leads?page=1&page_size=20
Authorization: Partner abc123...
```

schema.title: Response Get Partner Leads Crm Partner Leads Get

get.description:
Get contacts filtered by partner's allowed marketing sources.

Partners can ONLY retrieve contacts that match their configured
`allowed_marketing_sources`. This filter is automatically applied.

**Authorization:** `Partner <token>`

**Example:**
```
GET /crm/partner/contacts?page=1&page_size=20
Authorization: Partner abc123...
```

schema.title: Response Get Partner Contacts Crm Partner Contacts Get

post.description:
Privacy-conscious person lookup for partners.

Partners can check if a person exists in the system by email or phone.
Returns activity metrics without exposing PII.

**Important:** This endpoint does NOT filter by marketing source.
It returns whether the person exists anywhere in the tenant's data.
This is by design for duplicate detection purposes.

**Authorization:** `Partner <token>`

**Request Body:**
```json
{
    "email": "person@example.com",
    "phone": "+14155551234"
}
```

**Response:** Same as `/crm/lookup/person` - privacy-safe journey info.

Slim lead check for partners.

Quick check to determine if a lead should be submitted.
Returns minimal info: exists, name, and accept/reject recommendation.

**Logic:**
- **Reject** if person exists AND has activity within the inactivity window
- **Accept** otherwise (person doesn't exist, or exists but inactive)

**Bifurcation Logic (Internal):**
This endpoint transparently handles both Ambivo CRM checks and external
callouts. Partners always call this same endpoint - the routing decision
is made internally based on the partner's configuration:

1. Partner calls POST /crm/partner/check (always the same API)
2. Endpoint retrieves partner config including `duplicate_check_callout`
3. IF callout is enabled AND has a URL:
   → Forward request to external endpoint (20s timeout)
   → External endpoint must return at least {"exists": bool}
   → Return the external response to partner
4. ELSE (callout not configured or disabled):
   → Use standard Ambivo CRM lookup (contacts + leads)
   → Return Ambivo CRM response to partner

This design ensures:
- Backward compatibility (API contract unchanged for partners)
- Transparent routing (partners don't need to know about callouts)
- Centralized logic (bifurcation in one place)

**Custom Callout Requirements:**
If `duplicate_check_callout` is configured for this partner:
- External endpoint must accept `email` and `phone` parameters
- Must return at least `{"exists": bool}` in response
- Has 20 second timeout (not fire-and-forget)
- Can optionally return `name` and `is_active_within_window`

**Authorization:** `Partner <token>`

**Request Body:**
```json
{
    "email": "person@example.com",
    "phone": "+14155551234"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
    "exists": true,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "is_active_within_window": true,
    "recommendation": "reject"
}
```

Push a lead to the tenant's partner lead buffer.

Partners use this endpoint to submit new leads. Depending on
the partner's `auto_accept` setting:
- If `auto_accept=True`: Lead is immediately imported to tenant's leads
- If `auto_accept=False`: Lead is held for tenant review

**Authorization:** `Partner <token>`

**Request Body:**
```json
{
    "name": "John Doe",
    "email": "john@example.com",
    "phone": "+14155551234",
    "lead_source": "Trikin",
    "description": "Interested in services",
    "external_id": "PARTNER-REF-123"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
    "partner_lead_id": "...",
    "push_status": "pending" | "imported",
    "imported_lead_id": "..." | null,
    "message": "Lead received"
}
```

get.description:
Get leads pushed by this partner.

Returns the partner's own pushed leads with their current status.

**Authorization:** `Partner <token>`

**Query Parameters:**
- `push_status`: Filter by status (pending, accepted, rejected, imported)
- `page_size`: Number of results (default 50, max 100)
- `page`: Page number (default 1)

parameters.name: push_status

schema.description: Filter by status: pending, accepted, rejected, imported
schema.title: Push Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: pending, accepted, rejected, imported

schema.title: Response Get Partner Pushed Leads Crm Partner Pushed Get

get.description:
Get analytics for partner's pushed leads.

Returns counts by status, deal metrics, and commission info.

**Query Parameters:**
- `time_period_months`: Filter opportunity and pipeline data to last N months (default 6, options: 3, 6, 9, 12)

**Time Filtering:**
- Opportunity metrics (deals_created, deals_closed_won, etc.): Filtered by `close_date`
- Pipeline metrics (pipeline_imported_count, closing_* buckets): Filtered by `imported_ts`

**Authorization:** `Partner <token>`

parameters.name: time_period_months

schema.description: Time period in months for opportunity/pipeline metrics (default 6)
schema.title: Time Period Months

parameters.description: Time period in months for opportunity/pipeline metrics (default 6)

get.description:
List partner leads for admin review.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>`

Returns all partner leads for the tenant with filtering options.

schema.title: Response List Partner Leads Crm Admin Partner Leads Get

post.description:
Accept, reject, or import partner leads.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>`

**Actions:**
- `accept`: Mark leads as accepted for import
- `reject`: Reject leads (with optional reason)
- `import`: Import accepted leads to tenant's lead collection via proper CRM flow

get.description:
List configured partner portals.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>`

Returns list of partners for admin management.

patch.description:
Update a partner portal configuration.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>` (must be tenant admin)

**Allowed Fields:**
- `name`: Partner display name
- `description`: Partner description
- `allowed_marketing_sources`: List of allowed lead sources
- `whitelist_ips`: List of allowed IP addresses
- `can_read_leads`: Permission to read leads
- `can_read_contacts`: Permission to read contacts
- `can_lookup_person`: Permission for person lookup
- `can_push_leads`: Permission to push leads
- `auto_accept`: Auto-accept pushed leads
- `commission_rate`: Commission rate
- `commission_type`: Commission type (percentage/flat)
- `commission_notes`: Commission notes
- `agreement_link`: Agreement document URL
- `partner_contact`: Partner contact info {name, email, phone}
- `tenant_admin_contact`: Tenant admin contact info {name, email, phone}
- `match_preferences_schema`: Dynamic form schema (max 6 fields)
- `duplicate_check_callout`: External duplicate check config
- `status`: Partner status (active/inactive/suspended)

**Example Request:**
```json
{
    "name": "Updated Partner Name",
    "allowed_marketing_sources": ["Source1", "Source2"],
    "can_push_leads": true,
    "status": "active"
}
```

get.description:
Get a single partner portal configuration.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>` (must be tenant admin)

Returns full partner configuration for editing.

Get statistics for partner leads.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>`

Returns counts by status for admin dashboard.

Get list of marketing sources for dropdown selection.

Proxies to goferapi `/entity/data` with `entity_type=marketing_source`.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>`

**Response:**
```json
{
    "sources": [
        {"id": "...", "name": "Joe&Co"},
        {"id": "...", "name": "Trikin"},
        ...
    ]
}
```

post.description:
Create a new marketing source.

Proxies to goferapi `POST /user/marketing_sources` with `action: "add"`.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>`

**Request Body:**
```json
{
    "name": "New Source Name",
    "description": "Optional description"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
    "success": true,
    "source": {"id": "...", "name": "New Source Name"}
}
```

Check if lead exists in CRM (for admin Advise feature).

This endpoint performs the same CRM lookup as `/crm/partner/check` but
accepts Bearer token (admin JWT) authentication instead of Partner token.

Used by the admin interface "Advise" feature to help admins decide
whether to accept or reject partner-pushed leads.

**Authorization:** `Bearer <user_jwt>`

**Logic:**
- **Reject** if person exists AND has activity within the inactivity window
- **Accept** otherwise (person doesn't exist, or exists but inactive)

**Note:** This endpoint does NOT support external callouts. It only
performs Ambivo CRM lookup (contacts + leads tables).

**Request Body:**
```json
{
    "email": "person@example.com",
    "phone": "+14155551234"
}
```

**Response:**
```json
{
    "exists": true,
    "name": "John Doe",
    "is_active_within_window": true,
    "recommendation": "reject"
}
```

At least one of email or phone must be provided.
Used by partners to quickly check if a lead should be submitted.

```json
        "movein_date": "25-JAN-2027"

}
```

PersonLookupRequest.title: PersonLookupRequest
PersonLookupRequest.description:
Request for privacy-conscious person lookup.

At least one of email, phone, id, or external_id must be provided.
Used by third-party integrations to check for duplicate leads
and track lead journey without exposing sensitive data.

exists.title: Exists
exists.description: Whether any matching record was found (lead or contact)

matched_on.title: Matched On
matched_on.description: Which identifier matched (email, phone, both, id, or external_id)

has_recent_activity.title: Has Recent Activity
has_recent_activity.description: True if person has activity within the configured threshold (default 30 days)

journey_stage.title: Journey Stage
journey_stage.description: Current stage in the journey (furthest stage reached)

first_seen_date.title: First Seen Date
first_seen_date.description: Earliest date this person entered the system (ISO 8601)

PersonLookupResponse.title: PersonLookupResponse
PersonLookupResponse.description:
Privacy-conscious response for person lookup with full journey tracking.

Returns the complete lead → contact → opportunity journey without
exposing sensitive business data like names, IDs, values, or specific statuses.

**Privacy Notice**: This response does NOT expose:
- Names, IDs, or any identifying information
- Actual comment content
- Owner/assigned user information
- Deal values or commission-sensitive data
- Tags or custom fields
